trimmedChitChat.startTrimmed: None — Naomi opens with introduction immediately at 00:00:00.
trimmedChitChat.endTrimmed: Final ~121s (01:26:44 to 01:28:45) — brief 'we'll do a follow up' exchange and personal goodbyes.

lessons.title: Foundations: Why Pelvic Floor Health Matters and How It Works
lessons.topicSummary: Dr. Amanda Simmons' background, prevalence of PFD (1 in 4 women), education gap, pelvic floor anatomy (superficial vs deep), functions, tone spectrum, A-frame house model, and core canister.

lessons.title: Incontinence, Bladder Habits, and Urgency Management
lessons.topicSummary: Urge vs stress vs mixed vs athletic incontinence, bladder mechanics, healthy bladder habits, bladder diary, quick flicks for urgency, retraining, and PFMT as gold standard.

lessons.title: Prolapse, Constipation, and Vaginal Pain
lessons.topicSummary: POP (41-50% on exam, 3-6% symptomatic), grading, pessaries, PFMT plus whole-body strength, toileting habits, squatty potty, constipation management, heavy lifting as protective for prolapse, and vaginal pain causes/treatments.

lessons.title: Pregnancy, Postpartum, and the Six-Week Rule Debunked
lessons.topicSummary: Diastasis recti, pelvic girdle pain, lifting heavy in pregnancy, birth prep, pushing strategies, debunking the six-week immobilization rule, and strain variability.

lessons.title: What Pelvic Floor PT Looks Like, Screening a Provider, and Initial Q&A
lessons.topicSummary: What a pelvic floor PT visit looks like, criteria for screening a high-quality PT (certifications, internal assessment training, holistic view, must incorporate strength training), red flags (Kegels-and-breathing-only PTs), Ascend resources, and initial Q&A on culture/body disconnect, standards in the field ('PT 2.0' vs underloaded traditional PT), litigation-driven blanket recommendations, and nervous system reconnection.

lessons.title: Q&A: Scar Tissue, Interstitial Cystitis, and Hypermobility
lessons.topicSummary: Scar tissue work after multiple surgeries (cupping, scraping, tissue mobilization), interstitial cystitis as a symptom cluster (not a single diagnosis), pelvic floor dysfunction and IC overlap, and hypermobility/proprioception/POTS connections.

notes: APPROVED 2026-05-01 with split option C. 6 lessons. Lesson 5+6 split at 01:10:47 (4247s) where scar tissue Q&A begins. All lessons contiguous: 0->841->1640->2534->3285->4247->5204.
